title: pilosella brachiata subsp. flagelliflora
intro: Pilosella brachiata subsp. flagelliflora, also known as a subspecies of the False Oxeye dandelion, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Eastern Europe, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Pilosella brachiata subsp. flagelliflora include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve digestive health, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and aid in the treatment of various skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use.
